 {"id":"bd-1rz0","title":"Core Responsive Reflow Engine (Zero-Flicker, Tailwind-Grade)","description":"## Goal
Make resize/reflow first-class: zero-flicker, atomic rendering, and deterministic behavior under resize storms.

## Scope
- Core reflow engine with atomic present + shrink cleanup.
- Resize stream coalescing + scheduler (adaptive + anytime-valid).
- Responsive layout primitives (breakpoints + auto UI height).
- Instrumentation and performance budgets.

## Deliverables
- Reflow pipeline changes in core/render/runtime.
- Formal scheduler model + decision rules.
- Test harnesses (unit, property, snapshot, E2E PTY).

## Tests / Validation
- Unit/property tests for buffer invariants and scheduler correctness.
- Snapshot tests for reflow stability across sizes.
- E2E PTY resize-storm scripts with verbose JSONL logging (size, timing, seed, checksums).
- Performance baselines (p50/p95/p99) with budgets and regression gates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No flicker/ghosting on resize.
- Deterministic output under fixed inputs.
- Documented budgets and failure modes.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] The resize scheduler has an explicit formal model (loss matrix + decision rule) and is implemented without hidden heuristics.
- [ ] Resize-storm PTY E2E tests produce high-signal JSONL logs and deterministic replays (seeded).
- [ ] Golden checksums + snapshots verify behavior across a size matrix (80x24, 120x40, and at least one tiny terminal case).
- [ ] Performance budgets exist for resize storms (latency + allocations) and are enforced via CI/regression gates.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.365845941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T03:32:20.618367975Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","layout","performance","render","ux"],"comments":[{"id":328,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- Current resize handling is debounced with a placeholder, which blocks continuous reflow.
- Goal: make the system feel like a responsive Tailwind web page during resize, with zero flicker/tear and deterministic output.

Goals
- Continuous reflow during resize (no placeholder).
- Atomic, single-writer rendering with sync output when available.
- Robust, formally reasoned resize scheduling and coalescing.
- Performance‑bounded behavior under resize storms.

Non‑Goals
- No reliance on external GUI toolkits or OS-specific resize events.

Testing
- Unit + property tests for resize scheduling and layout behavior.
- PTY E2E tests with resize storms and verbose logs.
- Golden snapshots and regression/perf tests.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22Z"},{"id":329,"issue_id":"bd-1rz0","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Master TODO (Granular)
1. Spec + invariants
- bd-1rz0.1: Define reflow invariants (continuous, atomic, bounded latency, no placeholder).

2. Formal scheduler (alien-artifact)
- bd-1rz0.2: Formal model overview.
- bd-1rz0.2.1: Change-point detection (BOCPD) for resize regimes.
- bd-1rz0.2.2: Control-theoretic frame pacing (PID/PI).
- bd-1rz0.2.3: Anytime-valid e-process decision rule.
- bd-1rz0.2.4: Invariant proof sketches + failure-mode ledger.

3. Runtime integration
- bd-1rz0.3: Implement adaptive resize coalescer (depends on 2.1–2.3).
- bd-1rz0.4: Atomic reflow pipeline; remove placeholder.
- bd-1rz0.4.1: Atomic present protocol + shrink cleanup.
- bd-1rz0.4.2: Adaptive buffer reuse strategy.
- bd-1rz0.4.3: Inline reflow policy (auto vs fixed) tied to layout.

4. Responsive layout
- bd-1rz0.5: Responsive layout primitives.
- bd-1rz0.5.1: Breakpoint system.
- bd-1rz0.5.2: Auto UI height measurement + caching.

5. Compatibility + diagnostics
- bd-1rz0.6: Terminal capability strategy for tear-free output.
- bd-1rz0.7: Diagnostics + telemetry hooks for reflow.

6. Testing & verification (must be exhaustive)
- bd-1rz0.8: Unit + property tests (scheduler, coalescer, layout invariants).
- bd-1rz0.9: E2E PTY resize storm tests (verbose logs).
- bd-1rz0.10: Golden snapshots across size transitions.
- bd-1rz0.11: Perf regression tests + flamegraphs.
- bd-1rz0.14: Golden output harness + checksums.
- bd-1rz0.15: Resize storm generator + replay harness.

7. Performance methodology
- bd-1rz0.13: Baseline profiling + opportunity matrix before changes.

8. Docs
- bd-1rz0.12: Documentation + design rationale.

Notes
- Placeholder resizing must be removed; reflow is continuous.
- Present pipeline must be atomic and safe under shrink/grow.
- All tests must log detailed traces; no silent failures.

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.1","title":"Responsive Reflow Spec + Invariants","description":"Define core invariants: continuous reflow, max latency SLA, no placeholder, and atomic present rules.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Reflow Spec + Invariants
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Spec defines invariants, latency SLA, and atomic present rules with explicit non-goals.
- Includes unit-test plan for invariants (property tests + regression scenarios for resize storms, terminal shrink, and rapid mode switches).
- Includes E2E test script plan that logs resize events, frame timestamps, diff sizes, and flicker/ghosting detection into a structured JSONL report.
- Specifies required instrumentation points and log fields for post-run analysis.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:22.711423470Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:20:51.239292403Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T00:20:51.239273708Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["core","design"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.11","title":"Responsive Reflow — Performance Regression + Flamegraphs","description":"Benchmarks and flamegraphs for resize storms; enforce budget thresholds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Reflow — Performance Regression + Flamegraphs
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
- Baseline: record p50/p95/p99 with `hyperfine` and store command lines + raw outputs
- Profile: capture CPU flamegraph and (if relevant) allocation profile; include top-5 hotspots + opportunity matrix scores
- Regression gate: add a microbench/perf test with a documented budget and a JSONL perf log schema
- Logging: E2E logs are JSONL with stable schema (run_id, case, env, seed, timings, checksums, capabilities, outcome)
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:45:24.456917087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T14:51:07.531792486Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T14:51:07.531762780Z","close_reason":"Implemented performance regression benchmarks and tests for resize storms. Added:
- resize_storm_bench.rs: Criterion benchmarks for burst, oscillate, sweep, pathological, and mixed patterns
- resize_storm_regression.rs: Integration tests enforcing budget thresholds (avoidance ratio, memory efficiency)
- Exported resize_storm module from ftui-harness
- Added input_fairness module declaration to ftui-runtime lib.rs

All tests pass (13 regression tests + benchmarks)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1rz0.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1rz0.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1rz0.13","title":"Baseline Profiling + Opportunity Matrix","description":"## Task
Collect baseline performance metrics, flamegraphs, and an opportunity matrix before changes.

## Scope
- Benchmarks at key sizes (80x24, 120x40).
- Capture CPU and allocation profiles.
- Score hotspots by impact and effort.

## Tests / Validation
- Store raw outputs and command lines in JSONL.
- Record p50/p95/p99 and compare after changes.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Baselines are reproducible.
- Opportunity matrix guides optimization work.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:52:13.991490865Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T01:19:15.138168417Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T01:19:15.138150193Z","close_reason":"Completed: baseline benchmarks captured for ftui-render and ftui-layout at 80x24/120x40/200x60. Created opportunity matrix with top 3 recommendations (set/set_raw gap, fill SIMD, layout caching).  {"id":"bd-32x8","title":"Async Validation: Deadline Controller (Survival + Expected Loss)","description":"## Goal
Choose whether to cancel or wait for an in-flight async validation using a principled, latency-aware decision rule.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Model validation duration T with an online survival distribution. Use hazard h(t) and expected remaining time to evaluate expected loss:
- Survival: S(t) = P(T > t)
- Hazard: h(t) = f(t) / S(t)
- Expected remaining time: E[T - t | T > t] = (1 / S(t)) * integral_{t}^{inf} S(u) du

Decision rule:
Loss(wait) = L_stale * P(T > deadline) + L_delay * E[T - t | T > t]
Loss(cancel) = L_false_invalid + L_recompute
Choose action with smaller expected loss.

## Implementation Notes
- Track completion times per validator (EMA + variance).
- Approximate with a simple parametric family:
  - Weibull: S(t) = exp(-(t/lambda)^k), h(t) = (k/lambda)*(t/lambda)^(k-1)
  - Log-normal if tails are heavy.
- Expose config for loss weights and deadline budget.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Cancels only when expected loss of waiting exceeds cancel loss.
- Uses rolling window stats to adapt to slow validators.
- Never surfaces stale results after a newer input.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_cancel_when_long_tail: long-tail durations trigger cancel under tight deadline.
- unit_wait_when_fast: low-latency validator is not cancelled.
- unit_deadline_respected: validation state transitions to timeout cleanly.
- property_random_durations: decision matches expected-loss oracle within tolerance.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Simulated validators with controlled latency distributions; log decision + rationale.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-33xi","title":"Layout solver: Max constraint wastes space when item hits limit","description":"The layout solver has a known issue where Max constraints waste available space. When an item hits its Max limit, the redistributed space calculation is incorrect. Repro test exists at crates/ftui-layout/src/repro_max_constraint.rs. Root cause: when Max constraint is violated, space accounting doesn't properly handle the difference between allocated shares and actual consumed space.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:18:38.173826768Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T18:20:17.661678740Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T18:20:17.661659203Z","close_reason":"Test passes - bug appears to have been fixed in a previous commit","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
